channel.title: Manifolds on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Manifolds on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: Smooth Manifolds: Geometry Beyond Embedded Surfaces
item.description: Everything we have done so far — curvature, geodesics, the Gauss-Bonnet theorem — has treated surfaces as subsets of $\mathbb{R}^3$ . The surface inherits its metric from the ambient Euclidean space, tangent vectors are literally arrows in $\mathbb{R}^3$ , and normal vectors give us an extrinsic handle on curvature. This perspective is concrete and geometric, but it hides a deeper truth: most of the interesting geometry depends only on measurements made within the surface, not on how the surface sits inside $\mathbb{R}^3$ .
